meaning: adv.不适当地,不合宜地,笨拙地
examples: Abed and breakfast establishment inaptly named The Ritz is home toprison releases, homeless and immigration cases as well as a number ofaddicts.
Apart from a few new states that have become independent only since 1990, such as Eritrea and (de facto) Somaliland, the only laggard has been the inaptly-named Democratic Republic of Congo.
And he has vowed to cut the inaptly named "business privilege tax, " which takes 6.5% of any profits earned in the city plus 0.15% of the revenue even from a moneylosing enterprise.
Foremost among them is the inaptly cloying archness with which Vivian addresses the audience from start to finish ("It is not my intention to give away the plot, but I think I die at the end").
